reasoning: The community prediction was 1% in early April 2025, reflecting the very low base rate of new VOCs and the specific, stringent criteria. Since Omicron in 2021, no new Variant of Concern (VOC) has been designated by the CDC or the SARS-CoV-2 Interagency Group, as confirmed by current CDC publications. Furthermore, the requirement that daily incident hospitalizations reach 20,000 according to the COVID-19 Forecast Hub is practically impossible to meet because the CDC ceased requiring hospitals to report COVID-19 admissions on May 1, 2024, and paused their hospitalizations forecasts based on the Forecast Hub data. Given these structural barriers and that less than three months remain until the July 2025 resolution date, the probability of this occurring is at the lowest allowable threshold.
